summary: Audience tape of an underrated Father’s Day show – listenable but modest recording, with a relaxed set of nicely‑played classic Dead numbers.
review: The taping captures a lazy Sunday afternoon where the band stretched out on familiar material and delivered solid, if not spectacular, performances. Recording quality is described as “barely passable for the era” yet still listenable, with one reviewer calling it a good recording despite some mislabeled track info.

ai_rating.rationale: Four reviews give an average of 4 stars; comments are generally positive about the performance but note only modest sound quality, leading to a balanced rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as barely passable for its era yet listenable; one reviewer calls it a good recording, but track ordering and file labeling are problematic.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed Father’s Day set where the Dead took their time on typical songs, delivering a nicely‑played if not groundbreaking performance.

band_member_comments.Keys: Mentioned as Brent Mydland’s last show for many fans; his presence is noted fondly.
